mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT and PDGFR signaling to suppress AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutant AML; active in FLT3‑ITD+ lines including MOLM13

mechanism: Selectively inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases, blocking FLT3‑mutant AML survival signaling
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3+ AML; potent in FLT3‑ITD and TKD mutants including MOLM13/MV4‑11

mechanism: Highly selective inhibition of FLT3 kinase, leading to apoptosis in FLT3‑ITD AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; very strong antiproliferative effects in FLT3‑ITD lines such as MOLM13 and MV4‑11

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinases as well as VEGFR, PDGFR and FLT3, reducing proliferative and survival signaling
additional_notes: Used off‑label in FLT3‑ITD AML; widely available clinical drug

mechanism: Blocks SRC family kinases and ABL, and inhibits other kinases such as c‑KIT involved in AML cell signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML; shows reduced viability in some AML lines

mechanism: Targets BCR‑ABL, KIT and PDGFR tyrosine kinases to inhibit downstream proliferative pathways
additional_notes: Classic CML/ALL drug; some AML subsets with KIT or PDGFR rearrangements respond; less potent in MOLM13 and useful as comparator

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved in AML combination regimens; strong apoptosis induction and synergy with hypomethylating agents

mechanism: Inhibits Smoothened (SMO), blocking Hedgehog signaling implicated in leukemia stem cell maintenance
additional_notes: Approved with low‑dose cytarabine in AML; moderate direct antiproliferative effects in AML cells

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to reduce pathological JAK/STAT signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Used in myeloproliferative neoplasms; reported activity in subsets of AML and FLT3‑ITD/JAK‑activated contexts

mechanism: Blocks JAK1 and JAK3, dampening cytokine‑driven JAK/STAT survival signaling in leukemia cells
additional_notes: Rheumatoid arthritis drug; has shown activity in some leukemia models

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to suppress hyperactive JAK/STAT pathway signaling
additional_notes: Rheumatoid arthritis drug; similar rationale to ruxolitinib for hematologic malignancies

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4 and CDK6, enforcing G1 cell‑cycle arrest via the cyclin D–CDK4/6–Rb axis
additional_notes: Breast cancer drug; shows anti‑leukemic activity in subsets of AML and synergy with other agents

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4 and CDK6, blocking Rb phosphorylation and G1‑S cell‑cycle progression
additional_notes: Alternative to abemaciclib; only one of the two suggested for initial screening; reported 0.05–5 µM potency depending on line

mechanism: Allosterically inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and growth signaling downstream of PI3K/AKT
additional_notes: Targets hyperactive PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway in AML; associated with growth inhibition and differentiation in AML cells

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits BTK and also affects other kinases, perturbing B‑cell receptor–related and survival pathways
additional_notes: Approved for CLL/MCL; BTK and related signaling implicated in some AML subsets; modest direct AML activity but repurposing interest

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL and KIT, more potently blocking KIT‑driven signaling than imatinib
additional_notes: CML drug; shows antiproliferative effects in KIT+ AML and some activity in FLT3‑ITD models

mechanism: Inhibits multiple histone deacetylases, altering chromatin structure and gene expression to induce apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Strong anti‑proliferative and pro‑apoptotic effects in AML, including FLT3‑ITD lines

mechanism: Inhibits class I/II histone deacetylases, leading to hyperacetylation of histones and modulation of transcription
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved in CTCL; broad preclinical AML activity and widely used in AML combination studies

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing accumulation of misfolded and regulatory proteins and triggering apoptosis
additional_notes: Multiple myeloma drug; induces strong apoptosis in AML lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the chymotrypsin‑like activity of the 20S proteasome, disrupting protein homeostasis and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Second‑generation proteasome inhibitor; potent activity reported in AML cell lines
